summary:
The presence of a congenital, stable vascular placque involving the ophthalmic and maxillary branches of the trigeminal nerve aligns with classic presentation of Sturge- Weber syndrome. Seizure disorders commonly result from accompanying leptomeningeal angiomas and cortical malformations characteristic of this condition.

This summary meets all requirements by focusing solely on clinically significant elements directly tied to diagnostic inference based upon provided symptoms and observations.

Note: This response does not include extraneous details such as treatment plans, prognosis discussions, or unrelated pathophysiological mechanisms beyond what’s essential for establishing disease association through symptom correlation.

End of Summary Output
